figures.caption: Fig. 1: We deploy our policy in real-world environments, demonstrating agile forward and backward motion over discrete terrains that combine steps and gaps. The robot memorizes the surrounding terrain during forward motion and retrieves useful information from the memory state when moving backward, enabling highly dynamic backward locomotion using only a forward-facing depth camera.
TABLE I: Success rate (%) across difficulty δ ∈ {0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8} and protocol P1–P5.
Fig. 4: Real-world deployment on a robot equipped with a forward-facing depth camera. For clarity, all sequences are visualized such that the robot moves from left to right.

Fig. 2: Overview of the proposed LF2WB framework. We concurrently train a delta-rule estimator and an asymmetric actor-critic. The estimator learns a compact terrain memory with strong retrieval ability for backward motion, enabling the policy to execute dynamic backward maneuvers using only forward egocentric vision.

Fig. 3: RMSE of the body-centric elevation estimate along the executed path. The robot starts on the left of the image and walks backward to the right; 0 s corresponds to the leftmost position where the backward phase begins. Curve locations at terrain discontinuities are marked by arrows. Once the robot falls, we stop recording error.
